item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably because the things could dig through the walls/find a way in/-2 just straight up dying…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 but was wondering…Could 027-2 attract anomolous vermin like 3063?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To clarify, no, the vermin are not appearing out of thin air, but are attracted to 027 from the environment. See the "are drawn to" and "gather" statements.

Was going to upvote but why the fuck don't they put him in an airtight room

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you're taking it a bit too literal

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The post itself is fine but the title kind of rubs me the wrong way. The word God would imply that he created and/or can control said vermin but neither are true in the slightest. In my opinion, the most obvious route to take would be something related to the Pied Piper.

Edit: You could also give it a name relating to Moses seeing as it more resembles several of the curses brought upon the Pharaoh in the biblical story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love that this is an inheritable phenomenon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot, but what if there were more than one host at once? For all the foundation knows there could me more hosts out there, this could be like an infectious energy type thing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty much my worst nightmare. Just looking at many types of bug is enough to make me feel ill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems like it's the first thing, since a quick search gave me a lot of pages with this image, but no source, sadly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looking at Quik's edit comments, it looks like the image was commented out as its source could either not be found or was not compliant with the site license.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why was the image removed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice idea, but I think it would better look as a tale, not as an addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would be interesting to have a research note connecting this scp to the black death. For example the story of a man constantly attempting to flee the attraction effect and inadvertently exposing European population centres to higher levels of disease vectors.

item.title: Staff Post - Reversion Note (Open)
item.content:encoded:
Noting a reversion of a recent edit by NickWalker45, which involves the alteration of the following excerpt from:

Wherever the subject goes, an initial swarm of flying insects such as gnats and flies will start to form a cloud around him, usually within two to three minutes. Shortly thereafter, crawling animals (including lice, cockroaches, worms, spiders, [DATA EXPUNGED], mice, and rats)

To:

Wherever the subject goes, an initial swarm of flying insects such as gnats, flies, moths, mosquitoes and wasps will start to form a cloud around it, usually within two to three minutes. Shortly thereafter, animals numerous pests (including lice, fleas, cockroaches, worms, spiders, snakes, centipedes, frogs, possums, bats, [DATA EXPUNGED], mice, and rats)

Please note that this constitutes a content edit, and it requires approval from the author. For non-collab articles, non-authors may only edit errors in spelling, grammar and punctuation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A man covered in mice.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
timer removed

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 1 week if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ show block
– hide block

Only have a small edit suggestion, rewrite the passage "Shortly after this interview took place, Dr. Jameson and several other members of the research team for SCP-027 were transferred to other projects." as "Shortly after this interview took place, Dr. Jameson and several other members of the research team for SCP-027 were transferred to the SCP-1772 project."

SCP-1772 already has a researcher named Jameson and it's a sensible object to transfer someone who's just had a traumatic experience of their friend becoming an SCP. It's not terribly dangerous and the effect is busy enough allows them to focus on their work. I think it's a simple link that creates some great fridge logic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor guy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is extremely neat, but the elaborate suction system doesn't really make sense if they're being attracted from the environment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wonder if bacteria/viruses count as vermin. I mainly ask because vermin mainly means rodents to me. However, the definition does include insect pests. Would just be interesting to see if the host had a higher microbe count.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Note: Shortly after this interview took place, Dr. Jameson and several other members of the research team for SCP-027 were transferred to other projects.

This part is really sad. Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Creepy Crawlies all over me makes me +1 this and immediately look for another interesting scp to take my mind off of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're giving me a good niggle as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We're going to do everything we can to get 027 out of you. God, I… I'm sorry, K██████….

This is the Foundation, no? Am I to believe it can't get it's act together to create a containment cell that vermin can't get in to? Oh look, the rats are at Kevin again. If only we had access to research labs and decontamination procedures or.. I dunno… Specially created containment chambers.

If the vermin appeared as if by magic, this guys suffering would indeed be horrific. However as the author has said the vermin has to exist in the environment first, all you have to do is ensure there are no vermin in the environment and the job is done.

Also, what tests have been performed? What constitutes the environment? How far does the effect extend to?

It's a nice idea for an article, but those points niggle me.

and i need to go take a shower

of boiling acid

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great article, but how can research be 'inconclusive at best?' Isn't 'inconclusive' the absolute worst thing it can be?

item.title: Great Execution, Disgusting Idea!
item.content:encoded:
I love the attention to detail! My favorite part has to be the ending. So sad that the project workers were all transferred before they could help. Ugh, what a horrible existence, lol.

Nicely done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the fact that the Foundation is trying to study it, but literally has no idea what it is or what to study. It's a true anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. +1.

item.title: speculation
item.content:encoded: perhaps SCP 026 is a undetected strain of virus or contagion that needs rats and other insects to thrive. the original 'parent' being the only one able to infect a host

item.title: I agree
item.content:encoded: This did almost make me cry. That has to suck for this man to go through this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really do love this SCP. Apart from playing with some of my more irrational fears (bugs laying eggs in you? Oh my!) I really like the fact that there's just no escape from it.

+1
Who knew being eaten by rats could be so popular?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"a colony of rats nesting in the subject’s abdomen"

Sounds like a Ratgut from Brutal Legend lol

Anyway, +1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Creepiest part of this one? The hint that you could become this thing's next host. Better stay away from places with a lot of vermin, in case it's pulling them and about to kill its host…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very original and just the right tone of disturbing. I like how the bearer cannot actually control the animals and the reason why the animals are attracted is unknown. Also, great explanation of the 01.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at "Subject […] was discovered underneath this pile of rats."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting concept. Ill see what i can do.

And please double check your links.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suppose I should rephrase: it looks like an interesting Foundation tale for someone else to write. I'm too damn lazy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You certainly have my permission, as author of 997, to go ahead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I cannot imagine an upside to mixing those two SCPs.

At this line it occurred to me that this could make an interesting Foundation tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• Safe: If the Foundation were REALLY lucky it would only have the obvious effect - whoever was infected with 027 wouldn't know what was killing them, they would die, 027 would infect someone else. Repeat as long as an affected person is in range of 997.

NOTE: The Foundation is almost never lucky when it comes to crossing SCPs. Crossing SCP-914 and SCP-500 producing SCP-427 is a complete fluke. Of course, if you get too much exposure, you discover just how bad things can be…

  • EUCLID: The effect of 027 transfers to 997. ANYONE that is in range no longer detects the vermin that are eating them.
  • KETER: Both effects transfer to some of the vermin. And the effect is retained in future generations. Invisible flies, breeding in your nose. Undetectable lice, feasting in your hair. Roaches quite literally EVERYWHERE, including your food. Rats nibbling at your toes…

I cannot imagine an upside to mixing those two SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know we don't expose SCPs to each other, but I wonder what would happen if SCP-027 was exposed to SCP-997? Would it speed up the mental degradation if SCP-026-02 did not know he was attracting vermin?

What a horrible life. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting and something that some would consider a absolute nightmare.

Up voted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess that considering all the "WTF?" effects that go on at the Foundation, "will begin to appear" means something different to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To clarify, no, the vermin are not appearing out of thin air, but are attracted to 027 from the environment. See the "are drawn to" and "gather" statements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bah - things do not just "appear" in science! They "break from all established movement patterns and show a marked tropism to the location of the subject"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one is both creepy and sad. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So much better. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I changed the containment somewhat, based on chat feedback, and I'm open to other suggestions as well.

Added 1/16/10: Looks like a step in the right direction, and I think the next step would be an interview with the new host. Once I figure out the questions and answers, of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. It's good idea, but it seems a bit mundane.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, I think this needs some spicing up.
